executive_summary: Global systemic stability remains moderately stable despite significant energy sector volatility in the Middle East and elevated asset valuations within the artificial intelligence sector. Normalised supply chains and robust global fuel reserves are currently buffering these localised pressures and preventing immediate cascading disruptions.
correlations: The extreme volatility in Middle Eastern energy markets is being successfully absorbed by the optimal state of global fuel reserves which prevents the localised pricing spikes from translating into broader supply chain stress.
